history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because Task.java uses Hibernate 5 internal API CurrentTimestampFunction, which was removed in Hibernate 6 (bundled with Spring Boot 3).
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.x ships with Hibernate 6, which removed internal packages like org.hibernate.query.criteria.internal.expression.function. Adding org.openrewrite.hibernate.MigrateToHibernate61 to the Spring Boot upgrade step will rewrite the deprecated Hibernate 5 code to its Hibernate 6 equivalent before compilation, resolving the missing symbol error.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails because Task.java references org.hibernate.query.criteria.internal.expression.function.CurrentTimestampFunction, an internal Hibernate 5 API removed in Hibernate 6.
history.qwen_rationale: Splitting the Hibernate 6 migration into a dedicated step before the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade ensures the migration recipe runs against the Hibernate 5 classpath. This improves type resolution for the rewrite rules and allows deprecated internal API usage to be transformed before the dependency version bump triggers compilation errors.

history.qwen_observation: The MigrateToHibernate61 recipe changed javax.persistence imports to jakarta.persistence, but the jakarta dependencies weren't available yet since Spring Boot 3 upgrade hadn't run, causing compilation failures.
history.qwen_rationale: The UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe already handles javax→jakarta namespace migration and Hibernate 6 upgrade internally. Running MigrateToHibernate61 separately before it creates a dependency ordering problem where jakarta imports appear before jakarta dependencies are on the classpath. Removing the standalone Hibernate migration step lets the Spring Boot 3 upgrade handle everything atomically.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails during Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because Task.java references org.hibernate.query.criteria.internal.expression.function.CurrentTimestampFunction, a Hibernate 5 internal API removed in Hibernate 6.
history.qwen_rationale: Running MigrateToHibernate61 before UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 in the same step ensures Hibernate 5-specific code is migrated to Hibernate 6-compatible syntax while the classpath still contains Hibernate 5 artifacts. Immediately following it with UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 updates the parent POM and dependencies to Spring Boot 3.3 (Hibernate 6 + Jakarta EE), allowing the post-recipe compilation to succeed with the newly migrated source code.
